free_text: Neutron single-particle states in Nd-143 and Sm-145.
The elastically scattered particles
were detected in a 2 mm thick Si(Li) counter after
being analysed in a high resolution, magnetic
spectrograph. The solid state counter was placed in the
focal plane of the spectrograph and had a slit opening
sufficiently large to cover the whole elastic group.
Evaporated targets of a thickness of approximately
100 mu-g/cm2 of Nd-142 (>97%) supported by a thin
carbon film were used.

Fig.1
The elastically scattered particles
were detected in a 2 mm thick Si(Li) counter after
being analysed in a high resolution, magnetic
spectrograph. The solid state counter was placed in the
focal plane of the spectrograph and had a slit opening
sufficiently large to cover the whole elastic group.
Evaporated targets of a thickness of approximately
100 mu-g/cm2 of Sm-144 (>94%) supported by a thin
carbon film were used.
Angle digitizing error
Data digitizing error
Fig.2
A 30 cm scattering chamber equipped with a
horizontal turntable with a 2 mm thick Li(Si) counter
was used. The turntable was constructed in such a way
that the counter could be cooled. Another detector, a
0.8 mm thick surface barrier counter, was placed at a
fixed angle, 90 degr., and served as a monitor by
recording the elastically scattered deuterons.
The targets were self-supporting, metallic foils of
Sm-144 (> 94%)with a thickness of approximately
1 mg/cm2.

Tbl.3  p.490
Photographic plates placed along the
focal plane of the high resolution, magnetic
spectrograph at three different angles 90, 125 and 150
degr. were used.
An evaporated target of enriched Nd-143 (>91%)
supported by a thin carbon backing was used. The
thickness of the target was approximately 100 mu-g/cm2.
